lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... That's quite a few missing people... Looks like some of these have been here for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder if anyone around here knows more about this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Probably too much for me to take on right now though, but hopefully someone can find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should keep looking for these people...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. I don't suppose you had any more thoughts on the missing people?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: Not really. I'm sure if you keep asking around you'll find someone who can help you. Just don't waste your time on that funny guy by the sea...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Do you have a moment to talk?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know much about the missing people?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: Only what I've heard around the place. That funny guy by the sea keeps suggesting they're being taken in the night, which is absurd really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Funny guy by the sea?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: He's always going on about strange people coming in the night, but no one would dare do that with the ents around. It's a load of rubbish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't think it's possible?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: It would take a lot of things happening right before my eyes for me to even consider it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, what do you think is happening then?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: I think that some of them are accidents. Others might just be leaving.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No suspicion of anything else?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: Like what? Murder? I'm not sure who would want to kill folk around here. We're a tight community.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Thanks for the help, I guess.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Welcome to the Auburn Pub. Are you here to buy a drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the missing people...

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Ah, looking to find them, are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. What can you tell me about them?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Not a lot. Couple of kids that have run into trouble on some adventure no doubt, along with a few adults who decided to slip away in the night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that normal around here?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Well, we're sat between two sets of mountains. Lots of places for accidents to happen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The mountains... Right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anyone else who might be more... invested in the missing people?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Well, those posters aren't putting themselves on the notice board. You'll probably want to find whoever put them there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you don't know who that is?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Some local parents I think, but many faces come in here. I can't keep track of them all. Now, are you going to buy a drink?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Like I told you, you'll want to find whoever put those posters up. I think it was some local parents. Now, are you going to buy a drink?

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: You again? Come to laugh at me once more? I know the night people are real! You'll find out soon enough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: Not often someone comes out to speak to me. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the missing people. Do you know anything about them?

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: Oh, yes! Yes indeed! They were taken by the night people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The night people?

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: Yes! I hear them in the dark. They think they're quiet, but not to me! I can hear them scrape against the wooden walls!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could that not just be a dog scratching at the post?

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: Does a dog walk on two legs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One of the ents then?

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: An ent wouldn't go around scraping against things? They're far too careful for that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think-

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: Oh, I see what you're doing! You're just another who wants to laugh at me! You'll all see soon enough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... I think I'll leave you to it.

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: That's right! Be gone with you!

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Nilsal. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Nilsal. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the missing people...

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Ah, yes... It's a sad day when a town doesn't worry about its missing children...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: People aren't worried about it?

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: They were at first, but with how long it's been happening, I think everyone's run out of energy to keep searching.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But people have searched the? Are there any theories as to where they're going?

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Well, that depends on who you ask... Some try to rationalise it away as some accident in the forest, the river, or the mountains.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Others just assume they up and left overnight. Took only the essentials to go and start a new life.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Then of course, some think they're getting taken in the night...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what do you think?

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: I think we could be doing more. Something is happening and it doesn't feel right. I know I'm not the only one who feels this way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm here to have a look around. I'll see what I can find out about them.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Thank you, traveller.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Did you manage to find out anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: That's a shame... Hopefully you have some luck soon.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Good day, traveller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I was wondering if you had any more thoughts on the missing people?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: No. As I said, we tried looking, but found nothing. Without a new lead, things are at a dead end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Good day, traveller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I was wondering if you could tell me anything about the missing people.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Not really. We put a lot of effort into finding them, but nothing came of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you think they went?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Well, some say they got lost in the mountains. Others say they decided to go and live somewhere like Fortis instead of here. I'm not too sure myself. It is a large number of people who've vanished...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you stopped looking?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: It was taking up a lot of resources. We don't have a huge number of guards here, so I need to use my people wisely.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: If we get a new lead, I'm happy to send them out again, but right now it's like looking for a needle in a haystack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

sections.title: Talking to them again before talking to Ictus

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: You again? We already told you to go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I want to help!

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Prove it! What do you know?

sections.title: Talking to them again after talking to Ictus

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Have you found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Please, help us find our sons.

sections.title: Talking to them again after finding the scratches/puddles

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Have you found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are a few scratches on the wall outside along with this strange red substance.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Yes, I saw that, but I thought it was from the ents. Do you think something else left it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Possibly. It looks like there are puddles of the stuff leading away from your house. I'm going to follow them and see where they lead.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. Please, help us find our sons.

sections.title: Talking to Ariadna again after inspecting puddles

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Good day, traveller. Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Potentially. I was wondering if you have any clue what left these weird puddles through here?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Hmm... I hadn't noticed them before. I don't think the ents leave that sort of thing. My guess would be one of the woodland creatures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thank you for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me. I'm looking into the-

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Turn around. Walk out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, I-

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Please... Nobody's looking into anything. Just leave us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm trying to find the missing people!

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Prove it! What do you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Exactly! Now, go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I know most of the town has given up on finding them. Most are just dismissing the disappearances, assuming them to have run away or had an accident.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: However, there are some who think something more sinister is going on. If that's the case, I intend to find out. I'm [player name] and I promise I"m here to help.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: You... You'll find our sons?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to try.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Well... I suppose you do at least seem to somewhat care.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: What do you need from us, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: First up, what can you tell me about the disappearnce of your sons?

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: That's the thing... They just seemed to vanish! One moment they were playing upstairs, but the next they'd gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you didn't notice anything unusual at all?

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Nothing really. The only thing that was a bit odd was the ents...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The ents?

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Yes. Their normal path takes them right past our house, but the night the boys disappeared, they took a different route. It's like they were avoiding something...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Can I look around the house a little and see if there's any clues as to where your sons went?

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: If you think it will help, then of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's odd... There are some scratches going up the wall, along with a strange stick red substance. It's not blood... What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we have here? More of that strange substance. I wonder where it leads...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... A fishing rod might work...

sections.title: Using a fishing rod on the plank

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... I should see if this is from one of our missing people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think this plank can offer me anything else. I should go and see if that fabric is from one of the missing people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could this be from one of the missing people?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should I destroy this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It could be evidence of something happening here... Surely it wouldn't be ethical to do this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then again, I wouldn't want to give false hope to those parents if it's actually nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yet I could still ask them about it, even if I don't have it to hand...

sections.title: Talking to Aemilia or Francis after finding the fabric

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Have you found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: : Please, help us find our sons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I followed a trail of... well, I'm not sure what it is. It led from your home to the river, and I found a scrap of fabric. Does one of your sons often wear brown clothes?

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Yes, Etz does... What does it mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It means I may have a lead.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: What direction was this trail going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Up towards the mountains north west of here. It might be dangerous, but I think I can explore further.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: If you're willing to, it would mean everything to us. You're the only one who's actually cared enough to find anything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's the least I can do. I'll be back once I've discovered more.

sections.title: Talking to Aemilia or Francis again

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: You're back Did you find anything in the mountains?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Thank you for helping us. No one else seems to care...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay? Can you walk?

lines.speaker: Injured boy
lines.text: I think so... I'm not sure if Etz can though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's okay. I should be able to carry him. Now, let's get out of here...

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: I can't believe you found them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was very fortunate. They were in a cave in the mountains, but they weren't particularly far inside.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Probably using the survival skills I taught them! That's my boys!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are they doing now?

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: They're upstairs recovering. They need some rest, but they'll be okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good to hear, though I am still worried as to how they ended up there. It's not really the kind of place someone visits, not to mention that strange substance I found...

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: You think they were taken by something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think we can rule it out.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Well, they weren't the only ones to disappear. What if you're right? What if there are more people up there? And what if whatever took them comes back?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: First things first, I need to find out exactly what happened to your sons. Do you think I could ask them about it?

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Not yet. They need some rest first.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: In the meantime, you should go see Ariadna, the guard captain. People can't ignore this now. If something did take our boys, she needs to know about it. You'll find her by the main entrance into town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and see her. Is there anything else I can be doing to help as well?

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: : The boys were upstairs when they disappeared. What if whatever took them climbed our wall and came through the window? If that's the case, we need to stop it from happening again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's see... I suppose I could take a look at the wall and see if it can be reinforced in some way?

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: If it's not too much trouble, that would mean a lot to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure it won't be too much to sort. Right, I'll be back once I've spoken to Captain Ariadna and sorted your wall out.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: You're back. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got your wall all sorted. Now I need to go and see Captain Ariadna.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: She should be by the main entrance into town. Thank you so much for doing all this, iknami. It means everything to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to get your wall sorted. I haven't spoken to Captain Ariadna yet either.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: She should be by the main entrance into town. Thank you so much for doing all of this, iknami. It means everything to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got your wall all sorted. Now I'm helping Captain Ariadna source some new bows to equip the guards a bit better.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Thank you so much for doing all this, iknami. It means everything to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need your wall sorted. I haven't spoken to Captain Ariadna either.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: She should be by the main entrance into town. Thank you so much for doing all this, iknami. It means everything to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've helped Captain Ariadna source some new bows to equip the guards a bit better, and your wall is all sorted as well.}}

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Thank you, iknami. You'll be pleased to hear the boys are feeling a bit better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can speak to them now?

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Yes, but do be patient with them. They still have quite a bit of recovering to do.

sections.title: Attempting to go up the stairs

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: The boys are resting at the moment. You'll have to come back later if you want to speak to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now then... How do we reinforce you? Perhaps some maple logs will do the trick! I reckon I'll need four sets in total, and a hammer of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now then... How do we reinforce you? Perhaps some maple logs will do the trick! I reckon I'll need four sets in total.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now then... How do we reinforce you? Perhaps some maple logs will do the trick!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There we go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have those bows you asked for!

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Thank you, traveller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you've got the bows. Now what?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Well, not really a lot. We'll increase the number of guards posted at the entrances into town, but without knowing what we're looking for, we can't do much else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... Not quite the solution I was after, but it's a start. I guess I'd better go check in with those boys and see if I can find out more about how they ended up in that cave.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: You're back. Have you sourced those willow longbows yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite. How many did you need?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Just the four of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day! I'm here about those missing people.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Yes, I saw you bringing those boys back to town. Where did you find them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In a cave up in the mountains.

lines.speaker: Ariadna
lines.text: Did you see any sign of other missing people?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not, but I intend to ask the boys about them once they've had some time to rest. I'm worried they didn't go up there on their own. There's a chance something might have taken them.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Ah, wonderful. This is just what we need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I must admit, the town doesn't seem very well equipped to deal with this sort of thing. Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Well, one of the people who went missing was the local fletcher. In his absence, our already light supply of longbows has become even lighter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I can step in as your fletcher! New bows, was it?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: That's right. We typically use willow longbows. We've lost a few experienced archers over the years so we've tried to make things more approachable... and cheaper.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Willow... Approachable... Got it! How many did you need?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Four would keep us comfortable for the time being.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just so happen to have those on hand!

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Making the most of the woodlands around here, were you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Thank you, traveller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you've got the bows. What now?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: Ah, it's you. I hear you're still looking into the case of those missing people. Must say, you're the first person in a while to actually stick to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't like leaving a mystery unsolved.

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: Clearly. Anyway, good luck with it.

lines.speaker: Etz
lines.text: Please... find Antos.

lines.speaker: Shas
lines.text: He was deeper inside that cave. Those... things... they dragged him away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to head back there right now!

lines.speaker: Shas
lines.text: You're the one that saved us. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem. I'm just glad you're both okay. If it's alright, I wanted to ask you how you ended up in that cave. I'm hoping it might help me track down the others who are missing.

lines.speaker: Etz
lines.text: We didn't see any others... It was just us and Antos...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Antos?

lines.speaker: Etz
lines.text: Yes! He makes bows for the guards. We'd see him sometimes when dad would go with him to the pub, but he wasn't in the pub! He was in the cave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Shas
lines.text: He was deeper inside. Those... things... they dragged him away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't good. I need to get back there!

lines.speaker: Etz
lines.text: Please... find Antos.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Have you spoken to the boys yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: They're just upstairs, but do be patient with them. They still have quite a bit of recovering to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I need to get back to the cave. Apparently someone called Antos is still in there!

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Antos is up there? You'd better go and find him, iknami!

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Traveller. Did you find out more about what happened to those boys?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Well, without knowing what we're looking for, there's not much we can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I also found out someone called Antos is still in that cave! I need to go and find him!

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Antos? I'll get the guards ready, but that will take some time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We don't have time! I'm heading up there now!

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: You're going alone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's that or he dies!

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: You're mad, traveller! Good luck up there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Antos? Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Antos
lines.text: Behind... you...

sections.title: Talking to Antos after killing the strange creatures

lines.speaker: Antos
lines.text: Have they gone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For now, but there could be more. We should get out of here.

lines.speaker: Antos
lines.text: Thank you, iknami. I thought I could find out where people were going... No one else seemed to care... I managed to track those boys here, but then those things...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, Antos. The boys are safe.

lines.speaker: Antos
lines.text: That's good... but everyone else... Look what they did to them...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, Antos. We can't help them now, but at least you and the boys are safe. Come on, let's get you home, before more of those things arrive...

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: You found Antos! I never thought we'd see him again. What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That cave up in the mountains is full of some strange creatures. They were the ones responsible for the disappearances.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: I see... Did you find anyone else up there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. At least, no one alive, that is.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: That's... unfortunate. Still, from what you've said, I suppose we should be thankful that you found anyone still breathing.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Right, time to get to work I guess. Now that we know about these creatures and where they're coming from, we can hopefully ensure they don't take anyone else.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: I suspect it's going to require us to establish a constant watch. Not ideal, but doable, especially with those new bows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Well, if you ever fancy heading up there and killing more of those creatures, you won't find me complaining. Apart from that though, I think you've done all you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In that case, good luck with it all.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Thank you. All the best, traveller.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Hello again, traveller. We've been keeping an eye on the mountain pass to make sure those creatures don't come back. We haven't had any more disappearances, so it seems to be working.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good to hear.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Still, we can't be too careful. If you ever fancy going up to that cave and killing a few of them for us, that would be appreciated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might just do that.

lines.speaker: Captain Ariadna
lines.text: Thank you, traveller. All the best.

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: Thank you for finding our sons, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem.

lines.speaker: Francis
lines.text: Tetamo! You are too humble, friend. Without you, our boys would be dead. What you did means everything to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Aemilia
lines.text: No, thank you, iknami!

lines.speaker: Shas
lines.text: Thank you for saving us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't mention it.

lines.speaker: Etz
lines.text: And thank you for saving Antos as well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's really not a problem.

lines.speaker: Etz
lines.text: You're our hero!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: Ah, it's you. I hear you've become a bit of a hero around here. Not a bad job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: You found those people who went missing! Did you see them? Did you see the night people?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sort of. I wouldn't call them people though.

lines.speaker: Ictus
lines.text: But they're real! They're actually real! No one believed me, but I knew they were real!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, congratulations I guess...
